libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: When tests exercise Guice CGLIB/proxy/AOP paths, configure both JVM tests and native-image builds with `--add-opens=java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED`. Prefer public Guice APIs for coverage; the 2.9.1 jar relocates many internal util/cglib classes to names such as `com.google.inject.internal.util.$MapMaker` and `com.google.inject.internal.cglib.proxy.$MethodProxy`, so tests copied from 2.1.7 that import the old non-`$` internal class names will not compile.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8858 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.sonatype.sisu:sisu-guice:2.9.1; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: Adding `com.google.inject:guice` or other Guice artifacts to recover old internal class names would test the wrong implementation and can mask the actual `org.sonatype.sisu:sisu-guice` behavior.
`org.slf4j:slf4j-api` is optional in the POM and is not required for normal core Guice coverage unless tests intentionally exercise optional logging integration.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: The resolved 2.9.1 artifact has only normal transitive dependencies and needs no Docker image, but meaningful Guice proxy/AOP coverage on JDK 25 needs a module-opening JVM/native-image option.
